defs: To move with quick vibrations or undulations; as, a sail flutters in the wind; a fluttering fan.
To move about briskly, irregularly, or with great bustle and show, without much result.
quotes: No rag, no scrap, of all the beau, or wit, That once so fluttered, and that once so writ.

defs: To be in agitation; to move irregularly; to fluctuate; to be uncertainty.
quotes: Long we fluttered on the wings of doubtful success.
His thoughts are very fluttering and wandering.
